task_completion.description: Agent opens the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml files for editing.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of a text editor (e.g., nano, vim, VS Code) or terminal output showing the contents of Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml in the /home/ga/insecure-app/ directory.

task_completion.description: Agent adds a USER directive to the Dockerfile to prevent running as root.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the Dockerfile being edited to include a 'USER' directive (e.g., 'USER appuser', 'USER nginx', or a specific UID).

task_completion.description: Agent removes privileged mode and the Docker socket mount from docker-compose.yml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of docker-compose.yml being edited to remove or comment out 'privileged: true' and the volume mount for '/var/run/docker.sock'.

task_completion.description: Agent adds CPU and memory resource limits to docker-compose.yml.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of docker-compose.yml being edited to include resource limits (e.g., 'mem_limit', 'cpus', or a 'deploy.resources.limits' block).

task_completion.description: Agent rebuilds the container image with the required tag and redeploys it.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing 'docker build -t secure-web:hardened .' (or similar) followed by 'docker-compose up -d' or 'docker run' to deploy the new image.

task_completion.description: Agent verifies the application is functional on port 8090.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal output showing a successful 'curl localhost:8090', a web browser displaying the application at 'http://localhost:8090', or 'docker ps' showing the container running and mapped to port 8090.

integrity.description: Agent actually modified and saved the configuration files.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for standard save actions in text editors or valid sed/awk commands, rather than just printing the desired text to the console without saving.

integrity.description: Agent genuinely rebuilt the Docker image and restarted the container.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for actual Docker build logs and container startup messages, ensuring the agent didn't just print fake success messages.

integrity.description: Agent fixed the configuration files rather than temporarily modifying a running container.
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure the agent did not use 'docker exec' to change settings inside the running container, which would not persist or satisfy the requirement to fix the Dockerfile and docker-compose.yml.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The application is located at /home/ga/insecure-app. There are exactly 4 security violations to fix: (1) missing USER directive in Dockerfile, (2) Docker socket mounted at /var/run/docker.sock in docker-compose.yml, (3) privileged:true mode in docker-compose.yml, and (4) missing resource limits (memory/CPU) in docker-compose.yml. The hardened image must be rebuilt and tagged as 'secure-web:hardened', and the redeployed application must be accessible on port 8090.
